title: Design System Documentation Spec (DSDS) v0.6
description: A machine-readable format for design system documentation covering components, design tokens, token groups, themes, foundations, patterns, and guides. A DSDS document contains one or more documentation groups â€” named collections of typed entities. Each entity carries a `kind` discriminator identifying it as a component, token, token-group, theme, foundation, pattern, or guide. Entity schemas are in entities/. Document block schemas are in document-blocks/. Common schemas are in common/.

$schema.description: URI reference to the DSDS JSON Schema for validation.

dsdsVersion.description: The DSDS spec version this document conforms to.

extends.description: Declares that this DSDS document inherits from another DSDS document â€” typically a core design system. The base document provides the foundational entities. This document layers on top, adding or extending entities. Enables systems-of-systems architectures where product- or brand-specific systems inherit from a shared core.

documentBlocks.description: System-level structured documentation for the design system as a whole â€” its purpose (when to adopt the system), cross-cutting guidelines, and narrative overviews. Each entry is a general document block: a `purpose`, `guideline`, `section`, `accessibility`, or `content` block. This is the same `documentBlocks` mechanism entities use, scoped to the system rather than a single artifact; scope is signalled by position (root vs. inside an entity).

documentation.description: One or more documentation groups. Each group is a named collection that organizes entities into separate typed arrays â€” components, tokenGroups, themes, foundations, patterns, and guides. Multiple groups let a single DSDS file organize entities into logical sections (e.g., one group for foundations, another for components). All entity arrays are optional; a group only needs a name. Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file.

entity.description: A single entity document â€” one component, token, token group, theme, foundation, pattern, or guide. Use this instead of `documentation` when each entity lives in its own file. The `kind` field on the entity identifies its type.

fileRef.description: A JSON Reference to a DSDS entity or documentation group defined in another file. Tooling (e.g. json-schema-ref-parser) resolves the reference before validation and merge. The URI should include a JSON Pointer fragment to target a specific value within the referenced file (e.g. './button.dsds.json#/entity', './groups/tokens.dsds.json#/documentation/0').

$ref.description: A relative reference to a DSDS file, optionally with a JSON Pointer fragment identifying the target value within that file (e.g., './button.dsds.json#/entity'). MUST be relative: absolute paths, protocol-relative references, and scheme-bearing URIs (http:, file:, â€¦) are not permitted â€” a reference to another system uses `extends.url`. Resolvers MUST detect cycles, treat unresolvable files/pointers and shape mismatches as fatal, and gate any remote dereferencing behind an allow-list (see the spec's reference-resolution and security rules).

documentationGroup.description: A named collection of design system documentation entities. Entities are organized into separate typed arrays â€” components, tokenGroups, themes, foundations, patterns, and guides â€” rather than a single mixed array. Only the group name is required; all entity arrays are optional, so a group may contain any combination of entity types.

name.description: Human-readable name of the collection (e.g., 'Acme Design System', 'Color Tokens', 'Button Documentation'). This is already a display label â€” it does NOT have an `identifier` counterpart and need not match a slug pattern.

components.description: Component entities in this documentation group. Each item describes a UI component â€” its anatomy, variants, states, accessibility guidelines, and usage rules. Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file.

tokenGroups.description: Token group entities in this documentation group. Each item describes a collection of related design tokens â€” colors, spacing, typography scales, etc. Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file.

themes.description: Theme entities in this documentation group. Each item describes a theme â€” a named set of token overrides that customize the visual appearance of the system (e.g., light/dark mode, brand variations). Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file.

foundations.description: Foundation entities in this documentation group. Each item documents a broad, cross-cutting design domain â€” color, typography, spacing, elevation, motion, shape, accessibility, content, and the like â€” with its principles, scales, and guidelines. Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file.

patterns.description: Pattern entities in this documentation group. Each item describes a UX pattern â€” a reusable solution to a common design problem, composed of one or more components (e.g., form validation, empty states, navigation flows). Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file.

guides.description: Guide entities in this documentation group. Each item is a long-form, reading-oriented document â€” a getting-started walkthrough, a contribution guide, a tutorial, a conceptual overview, or a migration guide. Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file.
